label: Benchmarks, test suites & task metrics

node.label: Benchmarks, test suites & task metrics
node.definition: Benchmarks and task metrics define datasets, protocols, scoring rules, baselines, uncertainty, and reporting conventions for comparable evaluation.
node.why_it_matters: Benchmarks, test suites, and task metrics make model behavior comparable across systems, versions, datasets, and risk controls.
limitations: A benchmark can overfit to public examples, miss deployment-specific harms, or reward a proxy that diverges from the intended behavior.

dependencies.rationale: Benchmarks and task metrics use datasets, sampling, and train-test splits to define comparable evaluation cases.
Benchmark reports use statistics and experimental design to estimate performance, uncertainty, and comparisons.
Many benchmarks depend on labels, targets, rewards, or preference judgments as scoring signals.

sentence_units.text: Benchmarks and task metrics define datasets, protocols, scoring rules, baselines, uncertainty, and reporting conventions for comparable evaluation.
